scenario: A company uses model-driven app forms. You are designing a form that needs to show data from related tables and access HTTPS endpoints with client scripting. You need to optimize the performance of the form.
q: Which two actions should you take to meet the goal? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: Place controls in secondary tabs instead of the default tab.
Develop asynchronous event handlers to return a promise in the Onload event.
Use the XMLHttpRequest object with False as a parameter.
Place controls that are used the most at the top of your default tab.
answer: Place controls in secondary tabs instead of the default tab.
Develop asynchronous event handlers to return a promise in the Onload event.
why: To speed up form load, place controls (especially those bound to related-table data) on SECONDARY tabs - controls on non-default tabs are not rendered/loaded until the tab is opened, so the initial load is faster. And write ASYNCHRONOUS OnLoad event handlers that return a promise, so the HTTPS/related-data calls run without blocking form rendering. Using XMLHttpRequest with False makes the call SYNCHRONOUS (blocking) which hurts performance, and simply placing the most-used controls at the top of the default tab does not reduce the load work.
